steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and doubled last letter to intensify meaning
Added ة (taa marbouta) at the end to make it a feminine noun meaning 'the reality that will definitely happen'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific inevitable event
The ُ at the end shows this word is doing something in the sentence
Basic interrogative particle used to ask questions
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation. This word is asking about ٱلْحَآقَّةُ (The Reality) in this ayah.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show something becoming true
The alif after ح creates the active participle pattern فاعل
Adding ة at the end makes this word feminine and turns it from 'something that makes true' into 'the inevitable event/reality'
Adding 'al' makes it definite, like 'the' in English
The damma at the end shows this word is the subject of the sentence
This is an interrogative particle used to ask questions
The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer
Added و at the beginning to connect this question word to the verb that follows it (أَدْرَىٰكَ meaning 'made you know')
Basic meaning of knowing or understanding
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing vowels makes this a causative verb - showing someone making someone else know something
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) because this is a weak verb ending in ي

steps.m: he made you know (object of وَمَآ - 'and what')
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show 'you' as the object - the person being made to know. This word follows وَمَآ (and what) in the question 'And what made you know?'
This is a question word used to ask 'what?' about something unknown. In this ayah, it's asking about الْحَآقَّةُ (the Inevitable Hour).
Basic meaning of truth or reality
Adding vowel marks to make it a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb to a noun by adding alif after first letter, doubled the middle letter, and added ة at the end to make it feminine - means something that is definitely true/real
Added ال to make it specific and definite. The alif is written as آ when it comes after ال
Added ُ to show this word answers the question 'What is it?' (مَا) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This word relates to ثَمُودُ (Thamud) who are doing the action.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning intensive/causative - it's not just lying, but completely rejecting something as false

The ت at the end shows this action was done by either a feminine subject or a non-human plural subject. Here it refers to ثَمُودُ (Thamud), which is treated as a feminine singular noun since it refers to a tribe/group.
This is a proper noun naming an ancient Arabian tribe that rejected their prophet Salih
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject doing the action in كَذَّبَتْ (they denied/rejected)
This is a proper noun naming an ancient Arab tribe mentioned in the Quran
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: Ad (name of ancient Arabian tribe) (subject of كَذَّبَتْ)
steps.n: The word became a proper noun naming the ancient tribe. This tribe is one of the subjects of the verb كَذَّبَتْ (denied/rejected)

steps.m: and Ad (name of ancient Arabian tribe) (subject of كَذَّبَتْ)
steps.n: The prefix و is the conjunction 'and'. The fatha vowel (َ) is added to the و to enable proper pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he struck' to 'one who strikes' by using the فاعل pattern - like changing 'write' to 'writer'
Adds -ah ending to change from 'one who strikes' to 'the striking event' (like turning 'destroy' into 'destruction')
Added definite article ال to make the noun definite

steps.m: by the striking/devastating event (object of كَذَّبَتْ - they denied)
steps.n: Added preposition ب (by/with) and kasra ending to connect word as object in sentence. This word describes what was denied in كَذَّبَتْ (they denied).
This word comes from a basic particle rather than a normal root. It starts with the simple meaning of comparing or choosing between things.
Adding the shadda and alif changes this from a simple 'or' to a word that introduces a new topic
Adding the vowel marks helps show how to pronounce the word correctly
Adding فَ at the beginning connects this to what came before, like saying 'and' or 'then' in English

steps.m: Thamud (name of an ancient Arabian tribe)
steps.n: This is a proper noun referring to a specific tribe mentioned in the Quran that rejected their prophet Salih. It doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: Thamud (subject of فَأُهْلِكُوا - so they were destroyed)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence. The tribe Thamud is being talked about before the verb 'they were destroyed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show something was done to them rather than them doing it
The وا shows this is about a group (referring back to the people of Thamud mentioned earlier)

steps.m: so they were destroyed (as a consequence of their actions)
steps.n: The فَ shows this destruction was a result of their previous actions mentioned in the context
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past tense verb to noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Adds feminine ending to make it refer to a female transgressor
Adds genitive case ending to show it's being described or possessed
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds 'with' meaning and genitive case ending
This is a particle that introduces a new topic or contrast. It tells us we're about to focus on something specific.

steps.m: and as for (connects to previous ayah and introduces عَادٌ)
steps.n: The و connects this statement about عَادٌ (the people of 'Ad) to what was mentioned before

steps.m: to return, come back, or be old/ancient
steps.n: This is the name of an ancient Arab tribe mentioned in the Quran
Adding ا makes this the name of the tribe, pronounced as 'Aad

steps.m: Ad (the ancient tribe that was destroyed)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the subject of the sentence. This tribe was destroyed by a fierce wind as mentioned in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes to passive voice form to show the action was done to them (they were destroyed) rather than them doing it
Makes the verb plural masculine
The فَ (fa) prefix shows this happened as a result of something before, indicating sequence and causation
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'breeze/comfort'
Changed from general 'movement' word to specific word meaning 'wind' by changing middle vowel

steps.m: by a wind (used with عَادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا - Ad were destroyed)
steps.n: Added بِ to show 'by means of' and tanwin to show indefiniteness. This word explains how Ad were destroyed.

steps.m: to be intensely cold and make a howling sound (like a violent wind)
steps.n: This is a quadriliteral root that describes an intensely cold and noisy wind
This pattern makes it a noun describing the cold, noisy wind

steps.m: an intensely cold and howling (describing رِيحٍ - wind)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is describing the wind (رِيحٍ) mentioned in the ayah. Both this word and رِيحٍ have the same ending because they go together.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into a noun describing someone who does this action (like changing 'rebel' to 'rebel-er')
Makes the word feminine to match رِيحٍ (wind) which is feminine in Arabic. This word describes how powerful and uncontrollable the wind was.

steps.m: furiously strong (describing رِيحٍ/wind, owned by something)
steps.n: The ٍ (kasratan) at the end shows this word is describing رِيحٍ (wind) which is owned by or connected to something else

steps.m: to control or make something serve a purpose

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action was done with force or intensity

steps.m: he forcefully controlled it (referring to the wind mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what was being controlled (the wind, which is feminine in Arabic)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon (them). This connects to سَخَّرَهَا (He imposed it) to show who it was imposed upon.
Adding vowels makes it a proper number word that can count things
Connected the root letters together to form the basic word for 'night'
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple nights
Added a special ending mark because it's being counted by the number seven (سَبْعَ) before it
Changed from basic 'eight' root to the form used for counting things (like 'eight days')
Added -ah ending to make it the standard form of the number eight
Added fatha to show this is being acted upon

steps.m: and eight (connects to سَبْعَ لَيَالٍ - seven nights)
steps.n: Added و to connect this to the previous number (seven nights)
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a proper word
Added hamza at start to begin making it plural
Added the -in sound at the end to show these days are connected to another word that describes them
Added damma vowels to create a noun meaning the state of being completely stopped
Added tanween alif to show this describes how something happened. In this ayah, it describes how Allah imposed the punishment - in a consecutive way without breaks.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and the ى at the end shows it's happening now. The أ (hamza) drops because it's between two vowels.
Added فَ at beginning to show this seeing happens as a result of what came before in the ayah
Changed the root meaning from an action (standing) to a group of people who stand together, making it a concrete noun
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb ترى (you see) in this ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show the basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show what the preposition applies to. ها means 'it' and refers to something feminine mentioned before.
This form shows people who have been affected by the action - in this case, those who were thrown down and are now lying prostrate. The word describes 'the people' (القَوْمَ) in this ayah.
Starting with the basic comparison particle ك that shows something is similar to something else
Added أَنَّ which makes the comparison hypothetical, like saying 'as if' instead of just 'like'

steps.m: as if they (describing أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ - trunks of palm trees)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being compared. In this ayah, it refers to the people being described as fallen palm tree trunks.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning the end part of something
Forms plural noun meaning 'trunks' - specifically referring to the remaining bottom parts of palm trees in this context. This word is describing نَخْلٍ (palm trees).
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a usable word

steps.m: palm trees (like trunks of palm trees)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show multiple palm trees being described
Changes from verb 'to be empty' to describing something as 'empty'
Added feminine ending ة and genitive case ending ٍ to match the word it describes (نَخْلٍ/palm trees) in gender and case
This is a question particle that doesn't come from a root
The فَ shows this question follows from what was mentioned before. This word connects to تَرَىٰ (you see) in the rest of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you see (in response to فَهَلْ - 'then do?')
steps.n: Added ت at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. The ى with alif on top (ىٰ) is a special way of writing the end of this word that means the same as ى.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added ه with damma to show it refers to 'him'
Added م to change 'him' to 'them' (masculine plural)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added shadda (double letter mark) on م and kasra under it to emphasize the word. This happens because the previous word ends with a similar sound.
Added a small ن mark (called nunation) at the end. This helps connect the word smoothly to the next word in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: remaining/lasting (describes something that continues to exist)
steps.n: This form shows someone/something that is doing the remaining. In this ayah, it is the object (what is being seen) of the verb ترى (to see).
Adds ة to make it feminine

steps.m: any remaining/lasting thing (feminine, object of مِنْ)
steps.n: This word is the object of the preposition مِنْ (from/of) in the phrase 'Do you see of them any remaining thing?'
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make it past tense
Added alif after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word refers to Pharaoh (فِرْعَوْنُ) in this ayah.

steps.m: Pharaoh (the ruler who came before وَمَن قَبْلَهُۥ)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language
The ُ shows Pharaoh is the one who did the action of coming (جَآءَ)
This is a word that refers to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English. Here it refers to people who came before Pharaoh.

steps.m: and whoever (subject of came before فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous part, like saying 'and' in English. This shows that both Pharaoh AND others before him are being talked about.
Changes from verb 'to be before' into noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before him (referring to the people who came before Pharaoh)
steps.n: Added هُ to show 'him' and َ to connect it. The small ۥ shows how to pronounce the ending. This word is connected to مَن (who) in the phrase 'who came before him', referring to people before Pharaoh.
The word changed to show something that was flipped upside down or destroyed. The letter أ changed to و after the م at the start.
Made plural to refer to multiple cities. This word is the subject (doer) in the sentence along with Pharaoh, connected by 'and'.
Added the definite article ال to make the word definite

steps.m: and the overturned cities (subject with فِرْعَوْنُ)
steps.n: Added the conjunction و to connect with فِرْعَوْنُ (Pharaoh) as joint subjects. Added ُ to show this is doing the action.

steps.m: to make a mistake or sin

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changes from 'mistake' to 'someone who makes mistakes' by adding long aa and i
Adding ة makes it refer to the action or result rather than the doer
Adding the definite article makes it specific
Adding بِ shows how something was done

steps.m: with/by the sinful deed (object of جَآءَ)
steps.n: Adding kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is affected by the preposition بِ (with/by) that comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of a past tense verb.

steps.m: they disobeyed (رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ - the messenger of their Lord)
steps.n: Added وْا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ (the messenger of their Lord) which comes after it.

steps.m: and so they disobeyed (رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ - the messenger of their Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this disobedience was a result of what happened before
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
The َ at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb عَصَوْا (they disobeyed) - meaning they disobeyed the messenger
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the second letter to create the noun meaning 'lord'
Added kasra to show this word owns or possesses something
Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This word is connected to the next verb فَأَخَذَهُمْ (then He seized them)

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This action is a consequence of their disobedience mentioned in فَعَصَوْا۟.
Added هُمْ to show who was seized - refers back to those who disobeyed in the previous ayah

steps.m: so He seized them (as punishment for disobeying)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this seizing was a direct result of their disobedience mentioned in فَعَصَوْا۟
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a noun meaning the action itself

steps.m: a single seizing (of them by Allah)
steps.n: The ة shows it's one instance of seizing, and ً shows it's indefinite. This word describes how Allah seized the people mentioned in فَأَخَذَهُمْ (then He seized them)

steps.m: to increase or grow in size/intensity

steps.n: Feminine form ending in ة that means 'increasing/growing' - matches with أَخْذَةً (seizure) which is also feminine
Added ً to show this describes the indefinite noun أَخْذَةً
The doubled ر (with shaddah) makes the word stronger, showing how severe and intense the punishment was
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding an exclamation mark in English
The 'ا' at the end means 'we/us'
This particle adds emphasis to what follows
Adding مَّا (with shadda) changes this from a word meaning 'indeed' to a word meaning 'when' - it's like adding 'time' to the word

steps.m: when (specifically referring to طَغَا - it overflowed)
steps.n: When we add ا at the end, it means 'when' but specifically points to something that already happened in the past - like when the flood waters rose
Added fathas to make it past tense and show who did the action
The alif at the end shows this is a complete action in the past
The و (weak letter) changes to ا because it comes after a fatha
Adds a long alif (آ) to indicate intensity/emphasis
The word gets a damma mark at the end to show it's doing the action (it's the water that overflowed)
Adds ال to show this is talking about specific water - in this case, the flood water mentioned in the story of Prophet Nuh
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is the main verb describing what happened after the flood waters rose (طَغَا ٱلْمَآءُ)

steps.m: we carried you all (referring to humanity being carried in the ark)
steps.n: Added كم to show who was carried (you all). The alif in نا changes to ٰ when connected to كم. This refers to Allah carrying humanity in the ark (ٱلْجَارِيَةِ) during the flood.
This is the basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This preposition connects to 'the ship' (ٱلْجَارِيَةِ) to show what they were in.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense verb (it flowed) to active participle (something that is flowing)
Added feminine ending to create a noun for something that flows (i.e., a ship)
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ because it comes after the word فِى (in)
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Adding نَ at the start and changing vowels shows 'we' are doing the action in the present tense
Adding لِ shows this is being done for a purpose (to do something)

steps.m: so that we make it (referring to تَذْكِرَةً - a reminder)
steps.n: Adding هَا shows what is being made - it refers to the reminder (تَذْكِرَةً) mentioned later in the ayah
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced. This particle shows purpose or direction.
Added كُمْ which means 'you all'. This word is receiving the action of نَجْعَلَ (we make) in this ayah - it shows who the action is being done for.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ت at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra) makes it mean 'you are reminding' (present tense)
Adding ة changes it from 'the act of reminding' to 'something that reminds (like a lesson or message)'
This word is the object of لِنَجْعَلَهَا (that We may make it), showing what is being made into a reminder
Added vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he' to present tense 'you/she' by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Added ها to show what is being retained (referring to the reminder/تَذْكِرَة mentioned in the previous ayah)

steps.m: and she retains/preserves it (referring to تَذْكِرَة)
steps.n: Added و (and) to connect with previous phrase, and lengthened final alif for emphasis
Added damma vowels to create the noun pattern that means 'thing that does the action'. An ear is the body part that does the listening.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite (like saying 'an ear' instead of 'the ear'). This ear is described as one that retains or comprehends in this ayah.

steps.m: to keep or store something in mind

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ا after first letter and ة at the end to show this describes someone who does this action. This word describes أُذُنٌ (ear) in the verse.
Added tanween damma (ٌ) to show this is indefinite nominative, matching أُذُنٌ (ear) that it describes. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.
This is a compound particle made of فَ (so/then) + إِذَا (when). It introduces a conditional or temporal clause.
The word changes to passive voice, showing something was done to it rather than it doing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was blown (into الصُّورِ - the Horn)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that something received the action instead of doing it. This word connects to الصُّورِ (the Horn) which tells us what was blown into.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows that something happens 'in' the horn (الصُّورِ).
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is the standard spelling of this preposition in the Quran.
Added damma to create the noun for the trumpet that will be blown on the Day of Judgment
Added ال to make it specific and shadda because of sun letter ص
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition فِى (in)
This root represents the basic action of blowing or puffing air
Changed from basic root meaning 'to blow' into a noun meaning 'the act of blowing' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of blowing

steps.m: a single blast (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added two small marks on top (tanween) to show this is 'a' blast, not 'the' blast, and it's doing the action in the sentence
The ة makes this feminine to match with أُمَّة which is feminine
Basic vowel marks (fatha) added to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was alone'
Changes from verb (was alone) to noun (one who is alone) by adding alif
Added feminine marker ة because this word describes نَفْخَةٌ which is feminine
Added tanween damma (ٌ) to show this is nominative case like نَفْخَةٌ that it describes. The alif changed to an overextended alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he carried'
Shows that something was carried by someone else
Adds 'and' to connect with previous statement

steps.m: earth, ground, land (as a basic concept)

steps.n: Added marks (fatha and sukun) to make it mean 'the actual earth' instead of just the idea of earth

steps.m: earth (as the subject doing the action with الْجِبَالُ)
steps.n: The damma shows this noun is doing an action together with 'the mountains' (الْجِبَالُ) in this ayah

steps.m: the earth (as the subject doing the action with الْجِبَالُ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific earth we know, not just any earth
Changes from single mountain to many mountains
Added ال to make it specific mountains and ُ to show it's doing the action of being crushed in دُكَّتَا (were crushed)
Added وَ at beginning to connect it with the previous word الأَرْضُ (the earth), showing both were crushed together
Added shadda to show intensity of crushing action and connected letters with fatha
The fatha on first letter changed to damma to show something received the action

steps.m: they both (the earth and mountains) were completely crushed
steps.n: Added dual alif to show this refers to both الْأَرْضُ (the earth) and الْجِبَالُ (the mountains) from the previous ayah
Added فَ to show this crushing happens as a result of what was mentioned before

steps.m: to crush or level something completely

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong crushing action
Added ة to show one specific instance of crushing
Added -an ending to show this word receives the action of crushing
Changes from basic root to active participle form, meaning 'one'
Added feminine marker ة to make it refer to something feminine
Changed to show it describes how something was crushed (added -an ending to match دَكَّةً)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'day'
Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that particular day' being referenced

steps.m: so on that day (connects to وَقَعَتِ ٱلْوَاقِعَةُ - the Event will occur)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of something mentioned before. This word connects to the next phrase which talks about the Event occurring.
Added fathas to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added ت to make it feminine
Added kasra to make it 'you (feminine)'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show something that is happening
Added ة to make it a specific instance of the action
Added ال to point to a specific event (the one mentioned in وَقَعَتِ)
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb وَقَعَتِ (it occurred)
Added vowels and combined the doubled letter with shadda
The اِن prefix shows that something happens on its own without someone doing it

steps.m: it (feminine) split by itself (subject is ٱلسَّمَآءُ)
steps.n: The ت shows the subject is feminine (the sky/ٱلسَّمَآءُ)

steps.m: and it (feminine) split apart (subject is ٱلسَّمَآءُ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect to previous statement, and ِ at the end because it's connected to what comes after
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ا and ء at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the sky (subject of وَٱنشَقَّتِ - split apart)
steps.n: Changed spelling of 'the' (اَل to ٱل), stretched the middle letter to آ for emphasis, and added ُ at the end to show 'sky' is doing the action of splitting
This is a pronoun that refers back to السَّمَآءُ (the sky) which was mentioned in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: so it (referring to السَّمَآءُ - the sky)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is happening as a result of the sky splitting (وَٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being affected by something

steps.m: on that day (when the sky splits)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that specific day' being talked about in the verse when the sky splits

steps.m: to be weak, fragile, or fall apart

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha) added to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'it became weak'
